summary: Iteration 3 backend verification — snapshot persistence + admin attempts/recording/sharing endpoints. All 12 new tests PASS. Full regression: 37/38 pass; the single failure is the pre-existing TestOCR::test_ocr_extracts_questions due to a transient Cloudflare 502 from the LLM gateway (flaky, unrelated to Iteration 3 changes).

minor.issue: Flaky: returned 502 (Cloudflare Bad Gateway) during regression run. Likely transient LLM upstream issue, not introduced in Iteration 3.

verified_behaviors: POST /api/exams/snapshot — image_base64 IS persisted in proctor_snapshots; response strips image_base64 field.
Snapshot size cap — 250 KB input is truncated to exactly 200 000 chars; size_bytes reflects truncated length.
Data URL prefix 'data:image/jpeg;base64,' is stripped before storage; stored value matches raw base64.
GET /api/exams/admin/attempts — admin-only, returns attempts with violations_count (raw violations array removed), supports exam_id/student_id/status filters, sorted newest-first.
GET /api/exams/admin/attempts/{id} — returns attempt with snapshots_count; for submitted attempts also includes rank, total_participants, leaderboard (top 10), accuracy. 404 for unknown id.
GET /api/exams/admin/attempts/{id}/snapshots — returns snapshots sorted ascending by 'at', each includes image_base64.

POST /api/exams/admin/attempts/{id}/share — in_progress attempt -> 400 'Attempt not yet submitted'; student -> 403; submitted attempt returns share doc with id, channel, recipient, public_path='/r/{id}', certificate_path='/api/public/certificate/{id}', and message_template containing '{base}' plus both paths. share_events collection receives the doc (verified indirectly by 200 response and id presence).
critical_code_review_comments: routes_exams.py admin_share_attempt: `payload: dict = None` is non-idiomatic for FastAPI body — works because FastAPI parses an empty body to {} via the `payload or {}` guard, but explicit Pydantic model (ShareIn) would give validation, OpenAPI docs and type safety.

routes_exams.py store_snapshot truncates oversized base64 by slicing, which can corrupt the JPEG (invalid bytes at tail). Acceptable for proctoring artifacts but worth a comment or refuse-with-413 alternative.

admin_list_attempts uses .limit(limit).to_list(limit) but mutates each row to add violations_count — fine; consider Mongo $size aggregation to avoid pulling the full violations array if it ever grows large.
test_credentials: admin@gyansai.com/admin123 and demo/demo123 (TEST_ users created on the fly).
seed_data_creation: Per-test TEST_snap_* student is created + assigned to the seeded JEE Main exam, attempt started, snapshots pushed, then submitted in the share test. Student is deleted on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Iteration 3 endpoints are green. The new tests live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py classes: TestSnapshotPersistence, TestAdminAttemptsEndpoints, TestAdminShareEndpoint, sharing the module-scoped `submitted_attempt_setup` fixture. If TestOCR keeps flaking, consider marking it @pytest.mark.flaky or skipping when 5xx from LLM gateway.
